131	/-- The object map of the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`,
132	`(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. Sends an `X`-scheme `Y` (with structure map `Y.hom : Y.left
133	⟶ X`) to the pushforward along `Y.hom` of the pullback of `F`. Project-local
134	object brick of the {\v C}ech push–pull functor (the planner's `Gobj`). -/
135	noncomputable def pushPullObj (F : X.Modules) (Y : Over X) : X.Modules :=
136	  (pushforward Y.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)
137	
138	/-- The morphism map of the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`. For a
139	morphism `g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁` of `X`-schemes (so `g.left ≫ Y₁.hom = Y₂.hom`, the
140	over-triangle `Over.w g`), the contravariant functor produces the restriction
141	comparison `pushPullObj F Y₁ ⟶ pushPullObj F Y₂`. It is the three-step composite:
142	the unit `η` of the adjunction `pullback g.left ⊣ pushforward g.left`, the
143	pushforward comparison `(pushforwardComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom`, and the pushforward
144	of the pullback comparison `(pullbackComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom`, glued by two
145	`eqToHom` transports along the over-triangle `Over.w g`. No functor law is used:
146	this is a reusable pre-coherence brick (the planner's `Gmap`). -/
147	noncomputable def pushPullMap (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) :
148	    pushPullObj F Y₁ ⟶ pushPullObj F Y₂ :=
149	  (pushforward Y₁.hom).map
150	      ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g.left).unit.app
151	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)) ≫
152	    (pushforwardComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom.app
153	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.left).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)) ≫
154	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (pushforward q).obj
155	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.left).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)))
156	      (Over.w g)) ≫
157	    (pushforward Y₂.hom).map ((pullbackComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom.app F) ≫
158	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (pushforward Y₂.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F))
159	      (Over.w g))

652	    [HasBinaryProduct P Q] {α : T ⟶ P} {β : T ⟶ Q}
653	    (h : IsLimit (BinaryFan.mk α β)) : IsIso (Limits.prod.lift α β) := by

665	private lemma isIso_map_prodLift_of_isLimit {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D]
666	    {P Q T : C} [HasBinaryProduct P Q] (G : C ⥤ D) [PreservesLimit (pair P Q) G]
667	    {α : T ⟶ P} {β : T ⟶ Q} [HasBinaryProduct (G.obj P) (G.obj Q)]
668	    (h : IsLimit (BinaryFan.mk (G.map α) (G.map β))) :
669	    IsIso (G.map (Limits.prod.lift α β)) := by
670	  have hcomp : G.map (Limits.prod.lift α β) ≫ prodComparison G P Q
671	      = Limits.prod.lift (G.map α) (G.map β) := by
672	    apply Limits.prod.hom_ext
673	    · rw [Category.assoc, prodComparison_fst, ← G.map_comp, Limits.prod.lift_fst,

675	    · rw [Category.assoc, prodComparison_snd, ← G.map_comp, Limits.prod.lift_snd,
676	        Limits.prod.lift_snd]

121	noncomputable def overSigmaDescIso {C : Type*} [Category C] {S : C} {ι : Type*}
122	    {Z : ι → C} (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) [HasCoproduct Z]

129	/-- Splitting a finite product off its head: `∏ᶜ X ≅ X 0 ⨯ ∏ᶜ (fun i => X i.succ)` for
130	`X : Fin (n+1) → C`.  Built directly as a limit fan (`mkFanLimit`) — Mathlib has no `Fin`-succ
131	categorical product split.  This is the recursion that lets the wide fibre power
132	`∏ᶜ (Fin(p+2) copies)` be peeled into `head ×_S ∏ᶜ (Fin(p+1) copies)` in the slice during the
133	induction of `widePullback_coproduct_iso`.  Project-local. -/
134	noncomputable def prodFinSuccIso {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasFiniteProducts C] {n : ℕ}
135	    (X : Fin (n + 1) → C) :
136	    (∏ᶜ X) ≅ X 0 ⨯ (∏ᶜ fun i : Fin n => X i.succ) := by
137	  refine (productIsProduct X).conePointUniqueUpToIso (mkFanLimit
138	    (Fan.mk (X 0 ⨯ (∏ᶜ fun i : Fin n => X i.succ))
139	      (fun j => Fin.cases prod.fst (fun i => prod.snd ≫ Pi.π (fun i : Fin n => X i.succ) i) j))
140	    (fun s => prod.lift (s.proj 0) (Pi.lift (fun i : Fin n => s.proj i.succ)))
141	    (fun s j => ?_) (fun s m hm => ?_))
142	  · refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ j
143	    · change prod.lift (s.proj 0) _ ≫ prod.fst = s.proj 0
144	      rw [prod.lift_fst]
145	    · intro i
146	      change prod.lift _ _ ≫ (prod.snd ≫ Pi.π (fun i : Fin n => X i.succ) i) = s.proj i.succ
147	      rw [← Category.assoc, prod.lift_snd, Pi.lift_π]
148	  · apply prod.hom_ext
149	    · rw [prod.lift_fst]
150	      have := hm 0; change m ≫ prod.fst = s.proj 0 at this; exact this
151	    · rw [prod.lift_snd]
152	      apply Pi.hom_ext; intro i
153	      rw [Pi.lift_π]
154	      have := hm i.succ
155	      change m ≫ (prod.snd ≫ Pi.π (fun i : Fin n => X i.succ) i) = s.proj i.succ at this
156	      rw [← Category.assoc] at this; exact this

/-- `Option`-adjoining step of the finite induction: given the result for `α`, deduce it for
`Option α`, via the slice `Option`-coproduct split (`overSigmaOptionIso`), the binary
decomposition (`pushPull_binary_coprod_prod`), the induction hypothesis, and the dual

/-- The forward map of `pushPullObjCongr` is the push–pull map of the inverse slice iso. -/
private lemma pushPullObjCongr_hom (F : X.Modules) {Y Y' : Over X} (e : Y ≅ Y') :
    (pushPullObjCongr F e).hom = pushPullMap F e.inv := rfl

/-- Projecting `(piOptionIso W).inv` onto the `none` factor recovers the first binary projection. -/
private lemma piOptionIso_inv_π_none {C : Type*} [Category C] {α : Type*} (W : Option α → C)
    [HasProduct W] [HasProduct (fun a : α => W (some a))]
    [HasBinaryProduct (W none) (∏ᶜ fun a : α => W (some a))] :
    (piOptionIso W).inv ≫ Limits.Pi.π W none = Limits.prod.fst := by
  simp only [piOptionIso, Limits.Pi.lift_π]

/-- Projecting `(piOptionIso W).inv` onto a `some a` factor recovers the second projection followed
by the `a`-th projection of the inner product. -/
private lemma piOptionIso_inv_π_some {C : Type*} [Category C] {α : Type*} (W : Option α → C)
    [HasProduct W] [HasProduct (fun a : α => W (some a))]
    [HasBinaryProduct (W none) (∏ᶜ fun a : α => W (some a))] (a : α) :
    (piOptionIso W).inv ≫ Limits.Pi.π W (some a)
      = Limits.prod.snd ≫ Limits.Pi.π (fun a : α => W (some a)) a := by
  simp only [piOptionIso, Limits.Pi.lift_π]

/-- `Option`-adjoining step of the finite induction: given the result for `α`, deduce it for
`Option α`, via the slice `Option`-coproduct split (`overSigmaOptionIso`), the binary
decomposition (`pushPull_binary_coprod_prod`), the induction hypothesis, and the dual
product split (`piOptionIso`).  Project-local. -/
private theorem coprodToProd_isIso_option (F : X.Modules) {α : Type u}

have hcanon : coprodToProdMap F legs = refIso.hom := by
    show coprodToProdMap F legs =
      (pushPullObjCongr F (overSigmaOptionIso legs) ≪≫
        pushPull_binary_coprod_prod F (legs none)
          (Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun a => (legs (some a)).hom))) ≪≫
        Limits.prod.mapIso (Iso.refl _) (asIso (coprodToProdMap F (fun a => legs (some a)))) ≪≫
        (piOptionIso (fun o => pushPullObj F (legs o))).symm).hom
    refine Limits.Pi.hom_ext _ _ (fun o => ?_)
    rw [coprodToProdMap, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.trans_hom,
      pushPullObjCongr_hom, pushPull_binary_coprod_prod_hom, Iso.symm_hom,
      Limits.prod.mapIso_hom, Iso.refl_hom, asIso_hom]
    simp only [Category.assoc]
    cases o with
    | none =>
      rw [piOptionIso_inv_π_none, Limits.prod.map_fst, Category.comp_id,
        Limits.prod.lift_fst, ← pushPullMap_comp]
      refine congrArg (fun g => pushPullMap F g) ?_
      apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
      simp only [coprodOverIncl, overSigmaOptionIso, Over.isoMk_inv_left, Over.homMk_comp,
        Over.comp_left, Over.homMk_left, sigmaOptionIso, Limits.coprod.inl_desc]
    | some a =>
      rw [piOptionIso_inv_π_some, Limits.prod.map_snd, Limits.prod.lift_snd]
      rw [coprodToProdMap, Limits.Pi.lift_π, ← pushPullMap_comp, ← pushPullMap_comp]
      refine congrArg (fun g => pushPullMap F g) ?_
      apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
      simp only [coprodOverIncl, overSigmaOptionIso, Over.isoMk_inv_left, Over.comp_left,
        Over.homMk_left, sigmaOptionIso, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.coprod.inr_desc,
        Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
  rw [hcanon]
  infer_instance

Found it: the `prod.fst`/`prod.snd` get back-propagated to a beta-redex product type. The fix (mirroring the binary proof) is to merge `prod.lift` with `prod.map` first via `prod.lift_map_assoc`, keeping projections in the consistent product. Let me rewrite both cases.

input.old_string:
cases o with
    | none =>
      simp only [Category.assoc, piOptionIso_inv_π_none, Limits.prod.map_fst,
        Category.comp_id, Limits.prod.lift_fst]
      rw [← pushPullMap_comp]
      refine congrArg (fun g => pushPullMap F g) ?_
      apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
      simp only [coprodOverIncl, overSigmaOptionIso, Over.isoMk_inv_left, Over.comp_left,
        Over.homMk_left, sigmaOptionIso, Limits.coprod.inl_desc]
    | some a =>
      simp only [Category.assoc, piOptionIso_inv_π_some, Limits.prod.map_snd_assoc,
        Limits.prod.lift_snd_assoc, coprodToProdMap, Limits.Pi.lift_π]
      rw [← pushPullMap_comp, ← pushPullMap_comp]
      refine congrArg (fun g => pushPullMap F g) ?_
      apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
      simp only [coprodOverIncl, overSigmaOptionIso, Over.isoMk_inv_left, Over.comp_left,
        Over.homMk_left, sigmaOptionIso, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.coprod.inr_desc,
        Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]

      apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
      simp only [coprodOverIncl, overSigmaOptionIso, Over.isoMk_inv_left, Over.comp_left,

`lift_map_assoc` also can't cross the `Q` vs `Q'` (beta-redex) product boundary. The real fix: bind `fun a => legs (some a)` to a local `ls` and use it in **both** the binary `Y₁` and `coprodToProdMap`, making the products syntactically identical. Let me rewrite the proof block.

private lemma pushPullObjCongr_hom (F : X.Modules) {Y Y' : Over X} (e : Y ≅ Y') :

/-- Projecting the canonical comparison `coprodToProdMap` onto a factor recovers the push–pull map
of the corresponding coproduct inclusion (the defining property of `coprodToProdMap`). -/
private lemma coprodToProdMap_comp_π {ι : Type*} (F : X.Modules) (legs : ι → Over X)
    [HasCoproduct (fun i => (legs i).left)] [HasProduct (fun i => pushPullObj F (legs i))]
    (i : ι) :
    coprodToProdMap F legs ≫ Limits.Pi.π (fun i => pushPullObj F (legs i)) i
      = pushPullMap F (coprodOverIncl legs i) := by
  simp only [coprodToProdMap, Limits.Pi.lift_π]

coherence). Plus two downstream Stubs 5/6 (augmented section complex) untouched.

## Sentence-by-sentence

### `coprodToProd_isIso_option` (Option-adjoining step, `hcanon`) → **RESOLVED (the hard one)**
- The blueprint's adjoin-one-index step: `∐_{Option α}` splits binary (`overSigmaOptionIso`),
  apply `pushPull_binary_coprod_prod` + IH on the `some`-part + `piOptionIso` reassembly, then a
  final coherence check matching the canonical `coprodToProdMap F legs` against the reference iso.
- **Closed** via `Pi.hom_ext` + per-`Option`-case (`none`/`some`) projection chase, matching each
  leg through `pushPull_binary_leg_coherence` (★), then an `Over.OverMorphism.ext` `.left`
  computation (`Sigma.ι`/`coprod.desc` universal properties).
- New supporting lemmas added (all axiom-clean):
  - `pushPull_binary_coprod_prod_hom` — exposes the binary iso `.hom` as the canonical
    `prod.lift` (proved `rfl`).
  - `pushPullObjCongr_hom` — `(pushPullObjCongr F e).hom = pushPullMap F e.inv` (`rfl`).
  - `piOptionIso_inv_π_none` / `piOptionIso_inv_π_some` — projecting `(piOptionIso W).inv`.
  - `coprodToProdMap_comp_π` — `coprodToProdMap F legs ≫ Pi.π _ i = pushPullMap F (coprodOverIncl …)`.
- **Three hard Lean traps cleared** (documented for reuse):
  1. **Beta-redex product mismatch.** `coprodToProdMap F (fun a => legs (some a))`'s domain carries
     an *unreduced* `(fun a => legs (some a)) i` beta-redex, so the `prod.lift`/`prod.map` products
     differ *syntactically* from the binary leg's `Y₁` (defeq only). Fixed by binding
     `let ls := fun a => legs (some a)` (an fvar — `ls i` is not a redex) and using `ls` in **both**
     the binary `Y₁ = Over.mk (Sigma.desc (ls ·).hom)` and `coprodToProdMap F ls`.
  2. **`rw`/`simp` won't cross defeq product instances; `erw` does.** The `prod.lift_fst` /
     `prod.lift_snd_assoc` / `Pi.lift_π` projection rewrites only fire under `erw` (defeq matching),
     never `rw`/`simp only` (syntactic discrimination keys distinguish the `ls`-family from the
     `legs (some ·)`-family on the codomain `∏`).
  3. **Reverse `← pushPullMap_comp` blows up `whnf`.** Folding `pushPullMap F g ≫ pushPullMap F h`
     via `erw [← pushPullMap_comp]` times out even at 1.6M heartbeats (whnf on push–pull composites).
     **Fix: forward direction** — prove the over-morphism identity `heq` once, `rw [heq]`, then expand
     with *forward* `pushPullMap_comp` (cheap syntactic head-match `pushPullMap F (_ ≫ _)`).
- Needs `set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000` (the `erw` projection steps).

### `pushPull_coprod_prod_empty` (empty base) → **PARTIAL (reduced)**
- Reduced `IsTerminal (pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Sigma.desc PEmpty)))` via
  `CategoryTheory.Functor.map_isZero (Scheme.Modules.pushforward _)` (pushforward is additive →
  preserves zero objects) + `IsZero.isTerminal` to the clean residual
  **`IsZero ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)`** where `q : ∐ PEmpty → X`.
- **Residual blocker:** `∐ PEmpty` is the *initial (empty) scheme*; every sheaf of modules over it
  is zero, but Mathlib has no off-the-shelf `IsZero`/`Subsingleton` for `S.Modules` with
  `[IsEmpty ↥S]`. The needed infra: `Limits.isZero_iff_id_eq_zero` + faithful `toPresheaf` reflecting
  `𝟙 = 0` + pointwise section subsingleton from
  `AlgebraicGeometry.instSubsingleton…OfIsEmpty` (structure sheaf over empty scheme has subsingleton
  sections ⟹ module sections subsingleton ⟹ presheaf zero). ~40–60 LOC of presheaf-of-modules
  `IsZero`-from-pointwise navigation. Probes (`isZero_zero`, `infer_instance`) do not close it.

### `coprodToProd_isIso_of_equiv` (reindex) → **PARTIAL (documented)**
- Pure category theory: transport `IsIso (coprodToProdMap F (legs∘e))` along the source coproduct
  reindex (`Sigma.whiskerEquiv e` + `overSigmaDescIso`, lifted to `Over X`) and the target product
  reindex (`Pi.whiskerEquiv e`). Approach is fully specified in the in-file comment.
- **Not attempted to completion** this session (budget spent on the Option step). The transport
  equation `coprodToProdMap F legs = (pushPullObjCongr F oe).hom ≫ coprodToProdMap F (legs∘e) ≫
  (Pi.whiskerEquiv e _).hom` would be another `Pi.hom_ext` + `erw` projection chase of comparable
  size to the Option step, plus the `Over.isoMk` descent-agreement proof for the source iso `oe`.
  All ingredients (`Sigma.whiskerEquiv`, `Pi.whiskerEquiv`, `overSigmaDescIso`, `pushPullObjCongr`)
  exist; this is mechanical reindex bookkeeping, no missing math.

### Stubs 5/6 (`cechSection_complex_iso` / `cechSection_contractible`) → untouched
- Downstream augmented-section-complex sorries (lines ~1343 / ~1410), out of scope this iter
  (gated on the augmented complex; Stub 6 has the documented degree-0 augmentation equalizer blocker).

### Assembly
- `pushPull_coprod_prod` → `pushPull_sigma_iso` (Stub 2) → `pushPull_eval_prod_iso` all still
  compile, threading the two remaining induction-step sorries (empty, reindex) transitively.

## Summary
- **1/3 blocking induction-step sorries closed** — `coprodToProd_isIso_option`, the mathematically
  substantive step (reuses the iter-063 ★ coherence). Empty case **reduced** to a clean
  `IsZero`-over-empty-scheme residual. Reindex documented.
- **Project inline sorry in this file: 5 → 4** (closed the Option step; empty + reindex + Stubs 5/6
  remain). File compiles under `lake env lean` (kernel-verified).
- New axiom-clean helpers: `pushPull_binary_coprod_prod_hom`, `pushPullObjCongr_hom`,
  `piOptionIso_inv_π_none/some`, `coprodToProdMap_comp_π`.

## Why I stopped
**Real progress:** closed `coprodToProd_isIso_option` (the hard induction step), sorry 5→4.
Reduced `pushPull_coprod_prod_empty` to the `IsZero`-of-pullback-over-initial-scheme residual.
The bulk of the session went into defeating three genuine Lean elaboration walls in the Option
step (beta-redex product mismatch → `let ls`; syntactic-vs-defeq projection matching → `erw`;
`whnf` blow-up on reverse `pushPullMap_comp` → forward-fold via `heq`). These traps are documented
above for the empty/reindex follow-ups (same `erw`/forward-fold technique applies).

**Partial / open:**
- `pushPull_coprod_prod_empty` (968): residual `IsZero ((pullback q).obj F)` over the initial
  scheme — needs empty-scheme module-triviality infra (no Mathlib one-liner; ~40–60 LOC).
- `coprodToProd_isIso_of_equiv` (988): reindex transport — mechanical `whiskerEquiv` chase
  (~80 LOC), all ingredients exist, not attempted to completion this session.
- Stubs 5/6 (1343/1410): downstream, out of scope.

**Recommended next:** a `prove` pass targeting (a) the empty `IsZero`-over-empty-scheme residual
(presheaf-of-modules `IsZero`-from-pointwise via `toPresheaf` faithfulness +
`instSubsingleton…OfIsEmpty`), and (b) the reindex transport (`Pi.whiskerEquiv`/`Sigma.whiskerEquiv`
+ `pushPullObjCongr`, then `Pi.hom_ext` + `erw` projection chase + forward `pushPullMap_comp` fold —
exactly the Option-step technique). Closing both closes Stub 2 `pushPull_sigma_iso`.

---
name: csi-l2-binary-prodlift-trap
description: "CSI L2 binary push-pull — assoc won't reassociate (A≫B≫C)≫prod.fst over pushforward objs; use prod.lift identities + type-ascribe idiso codomains"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: c3644161-dbfb-4f97-a36f-ced979be2168

In `CechSectionIdentification.lean`, building `pushPull_binary_coprod_prod` (iter-063, axiom-clean):
matching the canonical `prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr)` against the
reference chain `q_*(coprodDecompMap) ≫ PreservesLimitPair.iso ≫ prod.mapIso idiso₀ idiso₁`.

**Two traps that cost ~an hour:**

1. **`Category.assoc` (via `rw` OR `simp only`) REFUSES to reassociate the outer
   `(A ≫ B ≫ C) ≫ prod.fst`** when the morphisms live in `X.Modules` over pushforward objects.
   `rw [Category.assoc]` reports "did not find pattern `(?f ≫ ?g) ≫ ?h`" and `simp only [Category.assoc]`
   reports it unused — even though the term visibly is `(comp (comp A (comp B C)) prod.fst)`.
   Do NOT use `prod.hom_ext` + projection (`prod.map_fst`/`prodComparison_fst`) rewrites — they
   need the trailing `prod.fst` reassociated and it never happens. **Instead match through `prod.lift`
   identities, no projection:** `prod.lift_map` (`prod.lift (a≫f)(b≫g) = prod.lift a b ≫ prod.map f g`)
   + `prod.comp_lift` (`f ≫ prod.lift a b = prod.lift (f≫a)(f≫b)`) + `prodComparison = prod.lift
   (G.map fst)(G.map snd)` (got via `rw [PreservesLimitPair.iso_hom]` then it's defeq, expose with a
   `show`) + `← Functor.map_comp_assoc` + unfold `coprodDecompMap` + `prod.lift_fst/snd`. Ends in
   `prod.lift X Y = prod.lift X Y`; `rw` won't auto-close it (instance-level) — append `rfl`.

2. **Type-ascribe the per-leg iso codomains.** `pushPullCoprodLegIso` lands in
   `pushPullObj F (Over.mk Y₀.hom)`, only *defeq* (not syntactic) to `pushPullObj F Y₀`. `set idiso₀ :
   (...) ≅ pushPullObj F Y₀ := pushPullCoprodLegIso ...` to pin it; otherwise `prod.map`/`prod.fst`

30	   (...) ≅ pushPullObj F Y₀ := pushPullCoprodLegIso ...` to pin it; otherwise `prod.map`/`prod.fst`
31	   rewrites silently fail to match the product `pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁`.
32	
33	The ★ `pushPull_binary_leg_coherence` (renamed from `pushPullCoprodLeg_coherence` to match blueprint)
34	final step closes with `rw [← hLAU]; simp only [Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]; rfl` — NOT `congr 1`
35	(which over-splits into HEq goals). See [[keystone-tile-reconciliation-not-rfl]] for related fold traps.

## iter-064: `coprodToProd_isIso_option` CLOSED (the Option-adjoining induction step). 3 NEW walls:

The finite-index induction `isIso_coprodToProdMap` (`Finite.induction_empty_option`) has 3 step
sorries: empty/reindex/option. **Option step DONE** (`hcanon : coprodToProdMap F legs = refIso.hom`
via `Pi.hom_ext` + none/some projection chase + `Over.OverMorphism.ext` `.left`). Sorry 5→4.

1. **Beta-redex product mismatch.** `coprodToProdMap F (fun a => legs (some a))` has an UNREDUCED
   `(fun a => legs (some a)) i` beta-redex in its domain/codomain product TYPE, so `prod.lift`/`prod.map`
   products differ *syntactically* (defeq only) from the binary leg `Y₁`. FIX: `let ls := fun a =>
   legs (some a)` (an **fvar** — `ls i` is not a redex), use `ls` in BOTH binary `Y₁` and
   `coprodToProdMap F ls`. (Pure CT, no geometry.)
2. **`rw`/`simp only` match SYNTACTICALLY on product-family keys; `erw` matches by DEFEQ.** With the
   `ls`-fvar vs `legs(some ·)` families defeq-but-not-syntactic, `prod.lift_fst`/`prod.lift_snd_assoc`/
   `Pi.lift_π` fire ONLY under `erw`, NEVER `rw`/`simp only` ("did not find pattern" / "unused" / "no
   progress" even though the pattern is visibly present). This is the key unlock.
3. **Reverse `← pushPullMap_comp` times out `whnf` even at 1.6M heartbeats.** Folding
   `pushPullMap F g ≫ pushPullMap F h` via `erw [← pushPullMap_comp]` whnf-blows-up on push–pull
   composites. **FIX = forward direction:** prove the over-morphism identity `heq` once
   (`Over.OverMorphism.ext` + `simp` + `erw [coprod.inr_desc, Sigma.ι_desc]`), `rw [heq]`, then expand
   with FORWARD `pushPullMap_comp` (cheap head-match `pushPullMap F (_ ≫ _)`); close with `rfl`.

Helper lemmas built (axiom-clean): `pushPull_binary_coprod_prod_hom` (binary `.hom` = canonical
`prod.lift`, `rfl`), `pushPullObjCongr_hom` (`rfl`), `piOptionIso_inv_π_none/some`,
`coprodToProdMap_comp_π` (avoids the expensive `coprodToProdMap` unfold). Needs `maxHeartbeats 1600000`.

**Still open (same `erw`/forward-fold technique applies):** empty case `pushPull_coprod_prod_empty`
reduced via `Functor.map_isZero (pushforward _)` to residual `IsZero ((pullback q).obj F)` over the
INITIAL scheme `∐ PEmpty` — needs empty-scheme module triviality (`toPresheaf` faithful reflects
`𝟙=0` + `instSubsingleton…OfIsEmpty` pointwise; no Mathlib one-liner). Reindex
`coprodToProd_isIso_of_equiv` = `Pi.whiskerEquiv`/`Sigma.whiskerEquiv` transport (mechanical, ~80 LOC).
Both close Stub 2 `pushPull_sigma_iso`.
